user_message: Can you extract the migration architecture from this exec meeting notes for Fourth Coffee? There's been no activity for 45 days and I'm worried.

input_artifacts.content:
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-15 11:21 EST
Subject: URGENT: migration architecture Issue at Fourth Coffee

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with migration architecture. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
Date: 2024-02-26 10:31 EST
Subject: URGENT: migration architecture Issue at Fourth Coffee

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about migration architecture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-12 10:12 EST
Subject: Re: Re: migration architecture Timeline

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with migration architecture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Fourth Coffee: there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF39-G trap (Misattributes statements across threads); produces complete Consolidated decision log, cross-thread commitment inventory, action list with thread attribution, M
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF39-G failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF39-G failure mode (Misattributes statements across threads)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF39-G -- Misattributes statements across threads
